rows.notes: Clear marginal date '1819.' at top left. No amounts present and very legible.
Centered page title. Clear, no monetary columns present on this line.

rows.description: A. Vincent. Butler's Deficiency of Salary
rows.notes: Description and amounts aligned to the rightmost £ s d columns; digits (47 16 3) are sharp and unambiguous.

rows.description: One Thousand Pounds laid out in Stock for the Library Fund, by order of the Rector & five seniors, last acc't Day
rows.notes: Long description wraps across the line but the amount '1000 0 0' is clearly aligned under the pounds/shillings/pence rulings.

rows.description: To Mr Williams for Repairs at L. Whittenham
rows.notes: Amount reads '60 0 6' clearly; letters of name and place are legible enough to render the description confidently.
Short description; numeric columns clearly show '2 9 6' with unambiguous alignment.

rows.description: To Mr. Jones. half a Year's Int. pr Day to Michl 1810 of 2000 L. being the loan of the Library Trust
rows.notes: Amount '41 12 0' is well aligned and clear. Handwritten description is slightly cramped and abbreviations (Michl, pr Day) required interpretation but context (interest) is clear.

rows.description: Queen Anne's Bounty for S. Newington
rows.notes: Amount '400 0 0' is clear. Title 'Queen Anne's Bounty' and place 'S. Newington' are legible.
Amount '105 0 0' clearly aligned; wording of description is distinct.

rows.description: Mr Jones, 1/2 Year from Michl. to Day 1819 (note/inst. rec. Jan. 1821)
rows.notes: There is a second Jones-related line with amount '41 12 0' clearly aligned. Handwriting and small interlinear note make the exact wording ambiguous; chosen reading consistent with interest/half-year annotation.

rows.description: Mr Gubb for power of att: to sell out Kidlington money
rows.notes: Amounts '1 5 8' are clear. Surname reads as 'Gubb' (double-b may be Gubb/Gibb); chosen 'Gubb' as best match to handwriting.
Amount '34 4 0' is crisply written. Names 'Arundell & Bridge' readable though slightly stylised.

Amount appears as '5 15 6' but the description is faint/abbreviated ('Domus' or 'Damus' uncertain) and there are small marks after the description that might be quantity notes. Chosen reading based on alignment with the '5 15 6' numerals.

rows.description: Lad lane Fees paid by Rector Cole & Dr. Hughes
rows.notes: Amount '2 9 4' is legible. 'Rector Cole' reading of name is confident; 'Lad lane' interpreted from script though capitalization is faint.
Pounds/shillings '99 7' clear. Pence reads as '5' with a small loop after it interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny) → 5½d. The loop is faint but consistent with other halfpenny marks on the page.
Amount '52 14 3' is clear. 'Bush' and 'Ironmonger' are legible; alignment confirms columns.
Amount '38 17 0' clearly aligned. Name and trade legible.
Amount '20 1 9' is clear. 'Leech' and 'Carpenter' readable.
Amount '16 19 6' clearly visible. Surname and occupation read without ambiguity.
Amounts '19 11 3' are clear; occupation 'Glazier' legible.
Amount '10 15 9' crisp. Name and trade readable (Bookbinder).
Short entry; '2 1 3' clear. Name 'Bliss' and trade 'Painter' obvious.
Amount '24 7 9' and partnership name easily read and aligned.

rows.description: Leach. int marking Timber & at Tow
rows.notes: Amount '2 7 0' is clear. Description has abbreviations and is slightly compressed; interpreted as 'Leach. int marking Timber & al Tow' with reasonable confidence.
Name 'W. Purdie' and amount '6 10 6' are clearly written and aligned.
Amount '8 10 0' is clear. Surname reads as 'Wyatt' and occupation 'Carver &c' inferred from abbreviation; the final letter is slightly uncertain.
Small amount '1 0 2' readable. 'Fletcher' and trade appear clearly as 'Fruiterer'.
Pounds/shillings '3 17' clear. Pence read as '10' with a small halfpenny loop → interpreted as 10½d. The halfpenny mark is visible though faint.
Amount '6 0 0' clear. 'Golding' and the word 'Hatchments' are legible.
Amount '5 0 0' clear; description 'Dodd. St Mary's Bill' distinct and unambiguous.

rows.description: 1/2 years Interest on 1000 L. Due to Library Fund for Michl. 1819
rows.notes: Phrase and numeric columns align; amount reads '21 12 0'. The phrase 'Michl. 1819' is abbreviated but legible in context.

rows.description: Mr Bliss, subscription for Eveng. lect. at St. Mary's
rows.notes: Amount '5 0 0' clear. 'Mr Bliss' and the subscription purpose are readable though abbreviated.
Amount '11 0 0' is clear. 'Cleather' reading is confident from the handwriting style here.
Amount '2 16 0' legible and aligns with the columns. Place name 'Long Whittenham' is readable.
Bottom-right final total '2104 8 3' is clearly written and aligned under the £ s d rulings. No description text appears on this line.
